notice: Onboarding and migration proof truth: every marketed switching row's packet binds no-account local entry, import diff/rollback posture, setup-later continuity, unsupported-gap taxonomy, first-useful-work success, and consumer-surface parity into one derived proof record whose effective claim narrows automatically when any dimension's evidence is stale, incomplete, or missing; no row may claim stable-grade switching without current evidence in every applicable dimension; stale evidence, incomplete rollback proof, or partial first-useful-work routing narrows the row to beta or preview with named reasons rather than inheriting an adjacent green row; and consumer surfaces (Start Center, migration center, Help/About, release notes, docs packs, support playbooks) ingest this index rather than cloning status text. Shell, docs, Help/About, support exports, and release packets read this index verbatim.

rows.label: Local open (no account)

no_account_local_entry.summary: Account-free local entry proven; no account or managed service required for local work.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Sign-in, trust, extensions, and remote connection deferred without blocking local work.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Local open does not involve an import from a source ecosystem.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.reason: No source ecosystem import; unsupported-gap taxonomy is not relevant for local open.

first_useful_work_success.summary: First-useful-work reached without account; Start Center and plain open reach the local editor.

consumer_surface_parity.note: Consumer wiring is pending; surfaces should ingest the canonical packets listed rather than maintaining bespoke status text.

caveats: Consumer surfaces are not yet wired to ingest the canonical packets; they currently maintain bespoke status text. Wiring is the natural next step for each surface owner.

rows.label: Clone and open

no_account_local_entry.summary: Local entry after clone is account-free once clone completes.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Setup deferral does not block post-clone local work.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Clone does not involve a profile import from a source ecosystem.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.reason: No source ecosystem import; gap taxonomy is not relevant for clone.

first_useful_work_success.summary: First-useful-work after clone is current but limited; archetype routing after clone is retest-pending.

caveats: First-useful-work routing after clone is limited; archetype-based routing stays retest-pending until the archetype qualification report exits retest-pending for the target archetype.
Consumer surfaces are not yet wired to ingest the canonical packets.

rows.label: Import from incumbent editor

no_account_local_entry.summary: Migration center rows stay available without account or managed services on all four ecosystem flows.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Completed import plus keymap with sign-in still deferring cleanly; setup-later continuity holds after import.

import_diff_rollback.summary: Diff is reviewed before apply and rollback is live for VS Code / Code-OSS; JetBrains, Vim/Neovim, and Emacs carry the same diff review but rollback evidence is incomplete, narrowing those flows to beta.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.summary: Exact/Translated/Partial/Shimmed/Unsupported taxonomy is visible before apply on all four ecosystem flows; every unsupported gap is disclosed before the user commits.

first_useful_work_success.summary: Import entry reaches first-useful-work with optional provider declined.

caveats: Rollback is live only for the VS Code / Code-OSS flow. JetBrains, Vim/Neovim, and Emacs flows carry reviewed diffs but no live per-ecosystem rollback evidence; those flows are narrowed to beta.

The entry-level import row reflects the most conservative ecosystem in scope. Consumers should present per-ecosystem claim levels from the migration center packet rather than a single aggregate entry claim.
Consumer surfaces are not yet wired to ingest the canonical packets.

rows.label: Restore from checkpoint

no_account_local_entry.summary: Warm restore from checkpoint stays account-free and does not require a managed service.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Deferred setup steps do not interfere with restore continuity.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Restore is a local checkpoint operation, not a source-ecosystem import.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.reason: No source ecosystem import involved in restore.

first_useful_work_success.summary: Restore landing reaches first-useful-work; partial restores declare exactly/partially/needs-review without implying full resumption.

caveats: Consumer surfaces are not yet wired to ingest the canonical packets.

rows.label: Missing root recovery

no_account_local_entry.summary: Missing-path entries stay visible without account; Locate recovery route is keyboard-reachable.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Degraded first-run state presents repair cues; recovery does not require a deferred setup step to complete.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Missing root recovery is a local entry recovery path, not an import flow.

first_useful_work_success.summary: Missing-target reopen reaches first-useful-work via the Locate route; no silent drop of the entry.

caveats: Consumer surfaces are not yet wired to ingest the canonical packets.

rows.label: Workspace switch

no_account_local_entry.summary: Workspace switch from Start Center stays account-free for local targets.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Workspace switch preserves prior state; deferred setup does not block the switch.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Workspace switch does not involve a source-ecosystem import.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.reason: No source ecosystem import involved in workspace switch.

first_useful_work_success.summary: Workspace switch reaches first-useful-work; cancel_switch and reopen_previous_workspace return paths stay available.

rows.label: Archetype-based routing

no_account_local_entry.summary: Archetype detection and readiness preflight stay account-free; auto-install and auto-trust are forbidden.

setup_later_continuity.summary: Set up later, Open minimal, and Dismiss recommendation are offered same-weight when setup is recommended.

import_diff_rollback.reason: Archetype routing is a detection and routing step, not a source-ecosystem import.

unsupported_gap_taxonomy.reason: No source ecosystem import in archetype routing.

first_useful_work_success.summary: Archetype detection delivers a readiness preflight record, but first-useful-work routing after archetype detection stays retest-pending until the archetype qualification report exits retest-pending.

caveats: Archetype detection is implemented and honest; first-useful-work routing after archetype detection stays retest-pending until the archetype qualification report exits retest-pending for each target archetype.
Shell, diagnostics, and Help/About are not yet wired to consume the archetype preflight record verbatim.

caveats: This archetype is labeled beta. The archetype qualification report must exit retest-pending before the claim may widen to stable.

summary.honest_posture: This is the honest posture for a pre-implementation repository: first-run local entry, restore, missing-root recovery, workspace switch, and VS Code / Code-OSS migration qualify Stable across all applicable dimensions; entry-level clone and archetype routing stay beta due to archetype qualification retest-pending; the three non-VS Code migration source rows stay beta due to incomplete per-ecosystem live rollback evidence; and all six archetype rows stay beta due to the archetype claim ceiling. Consumer surface wiring is universally pending and is the natural next step for each surface owner — consumer wiring does not narrow the core proof claims.
